title: Adoption risks & next product-quality improvement for pilot expansion
summary: Adoption is improving at the top of funnel (quickstart completion up after docs refresh), but expansion is currently blocked by a brittle setup/credential recovery experience that forces users into support and repeated failed states. For pilot expansion, the single highest-leverage product-quality improvement is to make failed-install/setup recovery self-serve and demonstrable (public checklist + reset/retry path), since it is both the largest analytics drop-off and the most visible demo failure mode.

claims.text: Quickstart completion increased from 44% to 58% after the docs copy refresh, with fewer exits at the first API call step.

The biggest remaining drop-off occurs during local setup and credential configuration, where users bounce after environment-specific errors; improvements disproportionately helped users who already had a valid token.

Support tickets cluster around environment drift, stale CLI versions, missing environment variables, and credentials copied from the wrong workspace; users often need an internal checklist that is not in public docs.
Several tickets reopen when users restart the quickstart and reproduce the same failed state, indicating lack of a recovery path after partial setup.

A cross-functional onboarding triage group agrees the post-partial-setup failure mode feels like a dead end and support wants a public recovery checklist covering stale CLI, credential mismatches, and repeated setup attempts.

openQuestions: Which specific setup error(s) drive the highest bounce during local setup (by OS/shell/CI context), and can they be reproduced reliably in the lab? [ties to S1, S6]
What are the exact steps in the internal support checklist that resolve most cases, and which require product changes vs. docs-only changes? [ties to S2]
What is the minimum observable signal in-product/docs that confirms a user has recovered (vs. silently remaining in a broken state) before retrying the quickstart? [ties to S2, S4, S6]
recommendation: Next product-quality improvement for pilot expansion: ship a self-serve “Failed install / setup recovery” path that users can complete without support—publish the recovery checklist and add clear reset/retry guidance for stale CLI versions and credential/workspace mismatches, including credential reset copy. This directly targets the largest remaining onboarding drop-off and the most visible demo brittleness, and it is explicitly a gating item before adding the next two pilot teams.
